business.title: Gold's safe-haven appeal fades as dollar strengthens
business.description: Gold plunged 11.3% in March, marking its steepest monthly decline since the 2008 global financial crisis. The ounce price fell to $4,099.52, its lowest since November 2025. Rising US dollar strength, higher bond yields, and liquidity needs drove the drop. Central banks' selling of gold also contributed. The precious metal had previously risen 12.42% in January and 8.9% in February.

business.title: Universities offer exam deferrals, tuition holds

science.title: In 2019, Scientists Discover a Fungus That Eats Gold. Some Already Want to Weaponize It for Space Mining
science.description: Most living things can't touch gold. It's chemically dead to them. But a common soil fungus found a way in, and now it wears the metal like armor.

science.title: Scientists found a protein that drives brain aging - and how to stop it
science.description: Scientists have uncovered a powerful new clue in the mystery of brain aging: a single protein called FTL1. In aging mice, higher levels of this protein weakened connections between brain cells and led to memory decline. But when researchers reduced FTL1, something remarkable happened — the brain began to recover, rebuilding lost connections and restoring memory performance.

science.title: Scientists trap light in a layer 1,000x thinner than hair
science.description: Researchers have created a nanoscale structure that traps infrared light in a layer just 40 nanometers thick—over 1,000 times thinner than a human hair. By using a unique material with exceptional light-bending properties, they can confine and intensify light far beyond previous limits. This setup also dramatically boosts light conversion effects, turning infrared into visible blue light. The advance could pave the way for smaller, faster photonic technologies.

ai.title: Trump-like fish species goes viral - but that's not the full story
ai.description: An image purporting to depict a 'new fish species' discovered in Venezuela is circulating online, as it bears a certain resemblance to US president Donald Trump. However, despite sporting spiky blonde hair, the animal is AI-generated, with TikTok user @flippers_peepers sharing the video on the platform in May 2025 with the hashtag #AI.
